source_paragraph: process, an audit involves acquiring and objectively evaluating evidence related to allegations of economic activities and other operations. The aim is to assess the level of conformity between the reports and predefined criteria, and to communicate the results to relevant users, in this case, the organization responsible for establishing information system gover nan nance. source_paragraph: PREVIOUS WORKS Among the relevant published studies in the log-based anomaly detection space, sequence-based is one of the most commonly used approaches. Therefore, there are limited relevant references as this study employs a point-based approach. Below are the relevant baselines from previous research: 1) Pylogsentiment is the first study which employs sentiment analysis-based anomaly detection on operat- ing system logs using GRU and GloVe embedding. 2) SentiLog , similar to , use a two-layered BiLSTM and GloVe embedding model to perform anomaly detection on parallel file system logs. 3) TransSentLog is a further development of and uses a two-layered transformer encoder which used two attention heads and GloVe embedding along with integrated gradients to add explainability to the trained model.

source_paragraph: I. INTRODUCTION I. INTRODUCTION The use of drones has become increasingly common in various applications, including aerial photography, surveying, and monitoring. However, drones can also be involved in incidents such as accidents, violations, or even crimes, making it necessary to investigate drone logs as part of forensic investigations. In a forensic investigation, drone logs are essential sources of information that can help investigators reconstruct the events leading up to an incident. Drone logs typically include information such as flight paths, altitude, speed, battery life, sensor data, and other telemetry information . source_paragraph: align with the anomaly severity context, where the distance between true and predicted labels matters. If the predicted label is lower than the true label, we define it False Positive (FP). Conversely, if the true label is lower than the predicted label, we define it False Negative (FN). In this context, having less FP is more important than having less FN, as the task objective is anomaly severity detection, where we aim to detect all abnormalities along with the severity level. Therefore, undetected abnormalities, or detecting higher severity as lower ones, indicate that the model fails to meet the task’s objectives. source_paragraph: can be fine-tuned into our dataset. V. CONCLUSION V. CONCLUSION This research demonstrates that the YOLOv8 model, specifically the YOLOv8m variant, provides excellent results in detecting Peritoneal Carcinomatosis (PC) from the used laparoscopic image dataset. By utilizing and fine-tuning the pre-trained YOLOv8 weights, this model shows superior performance with a mean Average Precision (mAP50) of 0.871, precision of 0.893, recall of 0.785, and an F1 score of 0.84. The predicted images also show that the performance of YOLOv8 with pre-trained weights is able to predict images well. All YOLOv8 scales are accurate in making predictions, with no errors in image prediction. In contrast, without using pre-trained weights, YOLOv8n and YOLOv8s still make errors in prediction, with some lesions being missed.
